page: The UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8570 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 24 physical cores providing 24 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation and a single thread per core. Memory capacity is set at 240.0 GB of RAM, yielding 10.0 GB per core. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU equipped with 192 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across multiple vectors. The system achieved a PassMark CPU score of 52754.5 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 47902.58. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4156.98 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 115.78 GB/sec. In decompression tasks, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance, recording a single-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 14.55 GB/sec. Large language model inference benchmarks for a small 135M model show top-tier processing speeds, reaching 9953.26 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 525.92 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a resource density perspective, the high ratio of memory to vCPUs combined with a massive 192 GB GPU frame buffer offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and parallel computing workloads. However, the shared CPU allocation and the complete absence of local storage represent clear architectural tradeoffs, requiring external storage solutions for persistent data. This server is optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads, including large language model inference, deep learning training, high-performance data science jobs, and complex database operations.
description: The UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a high-capacity GPU-accelerated server instance designed for demanding computational workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8570 processor with 24 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, the system is configured with 240.0 GB of RAM and a single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU featuring 192 GB of VRAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it utilizes a shared CPU allocation and lacks local storage. Benchmark results show strong performance, including a PassMark CPU score of 52754.5 and top-tier LLM inference speeds of 9953.26 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4156.98 GB/sec for cached reads. This instance is highly suited for deep learning, large-scale data science, LLM inference, and memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB VRAM, optimized for high-performance AI and data workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a KVM-based server with 24 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU for advanced AI and data workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity GPU instance combining Intel Xeon processing with NVIDIA Blackwell acceleration for demanding AI inference and memory-intensive computing.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8570 processor with 24 vCPUs and 240 GB of system memory
Single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB of dedicated VRAM
Top-tier LLM inference performance for prompt processing and text generation
High memory bandwidth with cached read speeds up to 4156.98 GB/sec
Shared CPU allocation on KVM hypervisor with no local storage included
Best suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and parallel data processing
